primary_meaning: a piece of furniture for sitting
usage_notes: Usually has four legs and a back
common_collocations: sit on a chair, wooden chair, desk chair
image_search_query: wooden chair with cushion sitting room
image_context: A wooden dining chair with a soft cushion in a cozy dining room
example_3: I need another chair for the table.
example_3_translation: Preciso de outra cadeira para a mesa.
pronunciation_tips: Starts with 'ch' like 'cheese', ends with 'air' sound
memory_hook: You 'chair' your friends at a table
common_mistakes: Don't confuse 'chair' (one seat) with 'chairs' (multiple)

primary_meaning: the entrance to a building or room
usage_notes: Can be opened and closed
common_collocations: open the door, close the door, front door
image_search_query: brown wooden door with handle house
image_context: A beautiful brown wooden front door with a brass handle on a house
memory_hook: A door 'does' the job of letting you in and out
common_mistakes: 'Door' and 'dore' sound similar but are different words

primary_meaning: an opening in a wall for light and air
secondary_meaning: a window on a computer screen
usage_notes: Glass window lets light in but keeps weather out
common_collocations: open the window, close the window, window seat
image_search_query: open white window sunny room curtains
image_context: An open white window with light blue curtains in a bright sunny room
example_3: I can see the garden from this window.
example_3_translation: Eu posso ver o jardim desta janela.
memory_hook: When you 'win' you can afford a window
common_mistakes: Don't confuse with 'wind' which sounds similar

secondary_meaning: a place where something grows or is placed
usage_notes: Go to bed = start sleeping, make the bed = arrange sheets
common_collocations: go to bed, make the bed, single bed
image_search_query: cozy bed with pillows blankets bedroom
image_context: A cozy bed with soft pillows and warm blankets in a peaceful bedroom
example_1: I go to bed at ten.
example_1_translation: Eu vou para a cama às dez.
example_3: Please make your bed every morning.
example_3_translation: Por favor, arrume sua cama todas as manhãs.
memory_hook: You go to 'bed' and put your head on the 'pillow'
common_mistakes: 'Bed' and 'bad' sound similar - be careful

usage_notes: Can be mobile (cell phone) or landline
common_collocations: mobile phone, answer the phone, call phone
image_search_query: smartphone with screen displaying contacts
image_context: A modern smartphone with a bright screen showing contact names
example_3: Please turn off your phone in class.
example_3_translation: Por favor, desligue seu telefone na aula.
pronunciation_tips: Long 'o' sound like 'show', no 'l' sound at end
memory_hook: You 'phone' a friend to talk on your 'phone'
common_mistakes: 'Phone' is short for 'telephone' - both are correct

primary_meaning: a vehicle with four wheels for driving
usage_notes: Also called 'automobile' or 'motor car'
common_collocations: drive a car, car keys, fast car
image_search_query: red car driving on highway sunset
image_context: A shiny red car driving on an open highway during a beautiful sunset
example_2: I drive to work by car.
example_2_translation: Eu dirijo para o trabalho de carro.
pronunciation_tips: Long 'a' sound like 'far', no 'r' sound at end in British
memory_hook: A car 'carries' you from place to place
common_mistakes: Use 'by car' not 'with car' for transportation

primary_meaning: a large vehicle that carries passengers
usage_notes: Public transportation - plural can also be 'busses'
common_collocations: bus stop, bus station, take the bus
image_search_query: city bus stopping at bus station
image_context: A yellow city bus stopping at a modern bus station with passengers waiting
example_1: I take the bus to school.
example_1_translation: Eu pego o ônibus para a escola.
example_3: Wait for the bus at the bus stop.
example_3_translation: Espere o ônibus no ponto de ônibus.
memory_hook: A bus is a 'big us' - it carries many of us
common_mistakes: Plural is 'buses' not 'buss'

primary_meaning: a vehicle that runs on tracks
usage_notes: Can be for passengers or goods
common_collocations: train station, by train, train track
image_search_query: modern train at railway station platform
image_context: A modern blue train arriving at a railway station platform with passengers
example_2: I go to work by train.
example_2_translation: Eu vou para o trabalho de trem.
memory_hook: A train 'trains' you to get to your destination
common_mistakes: Don't confuse 'train' (transport) with 'track' (rail path)

primary_meaning: the star that gives us light and heat
usage_notes: The sun rises in the morning and sets at night
common_collocations: sun rise, sun set, hot sun
image_search_query: bright sun in blue sky daytime
image_context: A bright shining sun in a clear blue sky on a sunny day
example_1: The sun is very bright today.
example_1_translation: O sol está muito brilhante hoje.
example_3: The sun rises in the east.
memory_hook: The sun 'sun' shines on everyone
common_mistakes: Use 'the sun' not 'a sun' when talking about our star

primary_meaning: the round object in the night sky
usage_notes: Goes through phases: new moon, full moon
common_collocations: full moon, moon light, new moon
image_search_query: full moon shining night sky stars
image_context: A beautiful full moon shining bright in a dark night sky full of stars
example_1: The moon is very bright tonight.
example_1_translation: A lua está muito brilhante esta noite.
example_2: I can see the moon from my window.
example_2_translation: Eu posso ver a lua da minha janela.
example_3: The moon comes out at night.
memory_hook: The moon 'moons' around the earth
common_mistakes: 'Moon' is singular for our moon - use 'moons' for other planets

primary_meaning: a light in the night sky
usage_notes: There are billions of stars in the sky
common_collocations: bright star, five stars, movie star
image_search_query: night sky full of bright stars
image_context: A dark night sky full of bright twinkling stars and a crescent moon
example_1: I can see many stars tonight.
example_1_translation: Eu posso ver muitas estrelas esta noite.
example_3: That movie star is very famous.
example_3_translation: Aquela estrela de cinema é muito famosa.
pronunciation_tips: Long 'a' sound like 'car' in American English
memory_hook: Stars 'star' in the night sky show
common_mistakes: Don't confuse 'star' (in sky) with 'stairs' (steps)

primary_meaning: the dark period between day and morning
usage_notes: Compare with 'day' and 'evening'
common_collocations: good night, every night, last night
image_search_query: city street at night lights
image_context: A beautiful city street at night with glowing street lights and lit windows
example_3_translation: A noite passada foi muito divertida.
memory_hook: Night 'knights' come out at night
common_mistakes: 'Tonight' is tonight, 'last night' was yesterday evening

primary_meaning: the early part of the day
usage_notes: From sunrise until noon or early afternoon
common_collocations: good morning, every morning, tomorrow morning
image_search_query: sunrise morning sunny park joggers
image_context: A beautiful sunny morning with joggers in a green park at sunrise
example_2_translation: Eu me exercito todas as manhãs.
memory_hook: Morning 'morns' the start of your day
common_mistakes: 'Morning' is AM, 'evening' is PM, 'night' is after dark

usage_notes: A year has 4 seasons and 12 months
common_collocations: this year, last year, next year
image_search_query: calendar showing new year dates
image_context: A calendar showing the dates of a new year with a celebration theme
example_1: This year I am learning English.
example_2: I visited my grandmother last year.
example_2_translation: Eu visitei minha avó no ano passado.
pronunciation_tips: 'Ye-ar' - in British, 'year' rhymes with 'here', American says 'year'
memory_hook: A year is 'ye-ar' long - a long time
common_mistakes: 'Last year' is previous year, 'next year' is the year coming

usage_notes: Used for counting and quantities
common_collocations: two times, two books, two friends
image_search_query: number two golden balloon
image_context: A shiny golden number two balloon floating against a white background
pronunciation_tips: Long 'oo' sound like 'too' and 'two' are homophones
memory_hook: Two is a 'two' - you and a friend
common_mistakes: 'Too', 'to', and 'two' all sound the same but mean different things

usage_notes: Used for counting objects or people
common_collocations: five times, five books, high five
image_search_query: golden number five balloon celebration
image_context: A bright golden number five balloon with sparkling effects
example_1: I have five fingers on each hand.
example_1_translation: Eu tenho cinco dedos em cada mão.
example_2: There are five apples on the table.
pronunciation_tips: Long 'i' sound like 'five' and 'hive' - rhymes with 'drive'
memory_hook: Five fingers on each hand - 'five' is alive with counting
common_mistakes: Don't confuse 'five' with 'four' or 'six'

usage_notes: A round number, easy to count in tens
common_collocations: ten times, ten years, count to ten
image_search_query: number ten golden balloon party
image_context: A large golden number ten balloon with party decorations around it
example_2: There are ten students in the class.
memory_hook: When you're 'tense', you count to 'ten'
common_mistakes: 'Ten' and 'tin' sound similar but mean different things

usage_notes: Your sister shares at least one parent with you
common_collocations: my sister, older sister, younger sister
image_search_query: two sisters smiling together holding hands
image_context: Two happy sisters holding hands and smiling at a birthday party
example_1: My sister is ten years old.
memory_hook: Your 'sister' is a 'sister' to you
common_mistakes: Don't call a female friend 'sister' unless in certain communities

secondary_meaning: a male member of a religious group
usage_notes: Your brother shares at least one parent with you
common_collocations: my brother, older brother, younger brother
image_search_query: brother and sister playing in park
image_context: A brother and sister playing together in a sunny park with a ball
example_2: I play football with my brother.
example_2_translation: Eu jogo futebol com meu irmão.
memory_hook: A brother brings another 'brother' into your life
common_mistakes: 'Brother' has 'er' at end, not 're' like 'father'

usage_notes: Babies cannot walk or talk yet
common_collocations: baby boy, baby girl, new baby
image_search_query: sleeping baby in crib with blanket
image_context: A peaceful sleeping baby in a white crib with a soft pink blanket
example_2: She had a baby last month.
example_2_translation: Ela teve um bebê mês passado.
memory_hook: A baby says 'ba-ba' - that's how the name came to be
common_mistakes: Don't say 'a baby boy' when you mean 'a baby who is a boy'

usage_notes: Plural is 'men', not 'mans'
common_collocations: young man, old man, every man
image_search_query: happy man smiling portrait outdoors
image_context: A happy smiling man standing outdoors with a beautiful background
example_3: All men and women are equal.
example_3_translation: Todos os homens e mulheres são iguais.
memory_hook: A man 'mann'ers make a man
common_mistakes: Plural is 'men' - 'mans' is not correct

usage_notes: Plural is 'women', not 'womans'
common_collocations: young woman, old woman, every woman
image_search_query: professional woman working at desk computer
image_context: A professional woman working at a modern desk with a computer
example_3: Women and men should have equal rights.
example_3_translation: Mulheres e homens devem ter direitos iguais.
pronunciation_tips: 'Woom-en' - the 'oo' sounds like 'book', plural has 'i' sound
memory_hook: A woman 'woos' men - 'woo-man'
common_mistakes: Plural is 'women' (ˈwɪm.ɪn) - 'womans' is not correct

usage_notes: We have two eyes
common_collocations: blue eyes, open your eyes, eye contact
image_search_query: beautiful blue eye close up view
image_context: A beautiful blue human eye in close-up view showing detail and color
pronunciation_tips: Long 'i' sound like 'time' and 'my' - 'i' alone sounds like 'eye'
memory_hook: You 'I' see with your eye - I and eye both start with 'I'
common_mistakes: 'Eye' and 'I' sound the same - context matters in writing

primary_meaning: the top part of the body
usage_notes: Contains the brain, eyes, ears, nose, mouth
common_collocations: head ache, head lights, from head to toe
image_search_query: human head silhouette profile
image_context: A clear silhouette of a human head and shoulders profile view
example_2: Shake your head yes or no.
example_2_translation: Acene com a cabeça para sim ou não.
example_3: The head of the school is kind.
example_3_translation: O diretor da escola é gentil.
memory_hook: Your head 'heads' your body - it's in charge
common_mistakes: Use 'headache' as one word, not 'head ache'

usage_notes: Opposite is 'cold'
common_collocations: hot day, hot water, hot food
image_search_query: hot cup of coffee steam rising
image_context: A hot cup of black coffee with steam rising on a wooden table
example_3: Be careful, the coffee is hot.
pronunciation_tips: Short 'o' sound in American English, more like 'a' in British
memory_hook: When it's 'hot', you say 'hoooo' to cool down
common_mistakes: Don't use 'hot' for weather that's just warm - use 'warm'
